title: [InstCombine] Restrain folding switch condition operation into case to one-use
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization by adding a **profitability guard** (`Cond->hasOneUse()`) before applying the switch condition inversion. Originally, the transform could invert the condition (e.g., XOR) and update case values, but this could increase code size if the condition had other uses. The generalization ensures the transform only fires when the condition is **dead after the switch**, preventing unnecessary recomputation or code bloat.

**Key takeaway**: When generalizing a pattern, identify and preserve the **profitability condition** from the original case. Here, the single-use check ensures the transform remains a pure simplification. Apply similar reasoning—ask if the transform truly reduces IR cost when the value has multiple uses.
